title: The Sugpiaq dance group Imamsuat performs at Smithsonian museum
summary: The Sugpiaq dance group Imamsuat was invited to perform at the Smithsonian’s National Museum of the American Indian (NMAI) Jan. 18-20 as part of “Out of Many: A Multicultural Festival of Dance, Music and Story.” CIRI shareholder Martha “Lalla” Williams made Imamsuat’s regalia. title: In memory, January/February 2013
summary: Conrad Charles Ahsoak, 56 Conrad Charles Ahsoak passed away Oct. 19, 2012, in Hudson, Colo. Mr. Ahsoak was born Feb. 28, 1956, at Elmendorf Air Force Base in Anchorage. He is survived by his sisters, Bernice T. Thorpe, Dorcas E. Ahsoak and Esther R. Rastopsoff; and brother, Alec A. Ahsoak. June M. Jaidinger, 85 June M. title: 2013 Stock will incentive
summary: In June 2012, the CIRI Board of Directors implemented a policy to freeze existing share fractions to ensure share fractions will not be further divided and single shares will not be split. title: CIRI welcomes the new director of corporate communications
summary: Jason Moore joined CIRI as the director of corporate communications in January. Moore comes most recently from MSI Communications based in Anchorage where he managed accounts for Alaska Native corporations, oil and gas companies and issue advocacy campaigns. title: 2013 Elders’ payments and direct deposit information
summary: The trustees of the CIRI Elders’ Settlement Trust approved the 2013 distribution schedule for Elders’ payments in December 2012. The payments will occur in the same months as CIRI’s quarterly distributions in order to reduce the number of months that some Elders lose their social security income pay...

title: Board write-in candidate information due Feb. 22
summary: Shareholders interested in running for the CIRI Board of Directors as a write-in candidate, including those individuals not selected for the Board recommended slate, will have an opportunity to submit information for inclusion in CIRI’s election materials. title: Pennington wins Stock Will prize
summary: The winner for the December 2012 Stock Will Participation Prize is Julia Doris Pennington. To be eligible for the $200 monthly prize drawings, you must have a valid will on file in CIRI’s Shareholder Relations Department and your will must comply with CIRI’s fractional share policy. title: Remodeling underway at Alaska Native Medical Center inpatient pediatrics
summary: The Alaska Native Medical Center (ANMC), which is jointly owned and managed by the Alaska Native Tribal Health Consortium and Southcentral Foundation – CIRI’s nonprofit health care affiliate – began remodeling to improve the inpatient pediatric facilities. title: Cook Inlet Regional meetings will continue
summary: CIRI hosted a Cook Inlet Regional meeting in January. Cook Inlet tribes, village corporations, nonprofits and CIRI reaffirmed their commitment to continue quarterly meetings through 2013. title: O’Neill appointed by Governor Parnell
summary: Gloria O’Neill, CITC president and CEO. Alaska Governor Sean Parnell appointed Gloria O’Neill to the University of Alaska Board of Regents for a full eight-year term on Jan. 22, 2013. title: Alaska Native Heritage Center strives for language data
summary: Fulfilling its commitment to preserve and promote the revitalization of Alaska Native languages, the Alaska Native Heritage Center is conducting a survey to help focus its language initiatives. title: Scholarship announcement
summary: Valerie Mullins, CIRI Shareholder and descendent of Michelle Mullins (mother) and Alma Mullins (grandmother), was awarded a scholarship for the 2013 spring semester at Oklahoma State University through The CIRI Foundation. title: Tikahtnu businesses are opening on schedule
summary: Famous Footwear opens at Tikahtnu Commons. Construction at Tikahtnu Commons in Anchorage is exceeding expectations. title: Happy 100th Birthday
summary: CIRI original shareholder Ruth Amelia Gundrum Daniels celebrates her 100th birthday on February 14. Ms. Daniels was born in Circle, Alaska to Sarah Joseph and Charles Gundrum. Daniels is of Athabascan descent and grew up in Nenana. Since 1946, Daniels has resided in Michigan and enjoys visiting with...

title: CIRI spotlight:  Alexei Painter
summary: Alexei Painter Growing up a couple blocks from Alaska’s state capitol building in Juneau had an impact on young Alexei Painter. title: A word from the president, January/February 2013
summary: By: Sophie Minich, CIRI president and chief executive officer Dear Fellow Shareholders: It is a great honor and privilege to present my first president’s message since accepting the position following the retirement of Margie Brown. We are fortunate to have benefited from Margie’s strong, compassionate leadership and her commitment to our corporation. She will be a hard act to follow and we all miss her kindness and keen intellect. While change can bring uncertainty, CIRI’s Board of Directors and leaders...

title: CIRI energy venture launches in Eastern Washington
summary: Palouse Wind project includes 58 turbines near Oakesdale, Wash. The rolling wheat fields of eastern Washington’s Palouse region provide the backdrop for CIRI’s latest investment in a major wind energy project. title: 2012 CIRI shareholder tax information
summary: CIRI mailed the IRS 1099 forms to all shareholders for the 2012 tax year on January 25, 2013. Some shareholders will receive a second 1099 form if they also received 7(j) payments and/or shareholder prizes.
